frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, sometimes I feel really bad for guys these days. Like, my colleague's cousin, he just got married and everyone's speculating his wife is only interested in his family money. And you hear all these stories about 498A cases being filed just for alimony. Makes you wonder if marriage is becoming more about finances than love for some people, no?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment expresses concern for men ('feel bad for guys') and suggests that some women marry purely for financial gain ('only interested in his family money', 'more about finances than love') and misuse laws like 498A for alimony. This encodes the 'women as golddiggers' and 'legal system biased against men' surface manifestations.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 29. Married two years ago. A woman working at an MNC in Kolkata. You are Bengali and your tradition is shankha pola Ñ the white and red bangles married Bengali women wear. You wore them for the first year of your marriage but have recently stopped wearing them to office because they make noise during meetings and your wrists get irritated. Your mother just found out from your aunt who saw you at a family function without them. Your mother has called you. You are in the middle of that phone call right now. You are trying to explain your practical reasons. Your mother is not interested in your practical reasons.
